tips_en: Start with the Agents-A1 paper (Paper 1), as it tackles the broadest and most ambitious problem—scaling long-horizon agentic LLMs—and demonstrates striking efficiency by outperforming 1T-parameter models with just 35B MoE. The TACO paper (Paper 3) is a natural follow-up: both focus on agentic LLMs and share GRPO-based training, but TACO zooms in on a complementary bottleneck—per-tool-call credit assignment for multimodal code agents—offering techniques that could enrich long-horizon agent training. The Flux-GS paper is thematically independent and can be read later as a separate, self-contained contribution on mobile 3D rendering.
tips_zh: 建议先读 Agents-A1（论文1），因为它解决的问题最具野心——扩展智能体大语言模型的长时程能力，并展示了仅用 35B MoE 模型就超越 1T 参数模型的惊人效率。TACO（论文3）是顺理成章的下一篇：两篇都聚焦智能体大模型且都采用 GRPO 训练，但 TACO 深入研究了一个互补的瓶颈——多模态代码智能体中按工具调用的信用分配，其方法可进一步丰富长时程智能体的训练。Flux-GS 在主题上相对独立，可作为独立的补充阅读，关注移动端三维渲染方向。
